results.title: Good time skiing
results.creator_credit: Bill Snider, Len Aitken and Crystal Productions
results.brief_description: Portrays cross-country skiing as a pleasure sport as well as an Olympic event. Pleasure skiers are shown traversing magnificent mountain country inaccessible to hikers and down-hill skiers; and skiers in turn of the century costume are seen using telemark turns to ski deep powder snow. The film also demonstrates techniques used by the Olympic team which will enhance the skills of, and provide additional pleasure to Nordic skiers. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Good record keeping
results.brief_description: This video provides information about the requirements and importance of good record keeping for the New Tax System, implemented July 1, 2000. Presented by Michael Caton. Covers and defines such topics as: Acquisitions ; Australian Business Number ; Business Activity Statement ; GST-free ; Input taxed ; Input tax credit ; PAYG instalments ; PAYG withholding ; Supplies ; Taxable supplies ; Tax period.

results.title: Good morning, Beijing = Beijing ni zao [NTSC]
results.creator_credit: Zhang Nuanxin, Beijing Film Academy and Youth Film Studio
results.brief_description: Directed by one of the few women to have become prominent in China’s film industry, this film depicts the head-on collision between socialist and market ethics in 1980s Beijing through the work and romantic lives of a group of young adults. The equilibrium between three friends who work as bus conductors is disturbed as they seize upon new challenges and opportunities that open up to them as a result of economic reforms. Cast includes Ma Xiaoqing and Wang Quan’an. In Mandarin with English subtitles.

results.title: Golf: history and tradition
results.brief_description: This dramatic video takes you on a pilgrimage to the heart and home of Scotland with a rare look inside the clubhouse at St. Andrews. Treasures of the past are revealed from feathery balls and longnose clubs, to the clubs in use today. See the development of golf course architecture by visiting the famous Link Courses.

results.title: Golf: 50 steps to improve your game
results.brief_description: Hosted by professional golf teacher Richard Bradbeer and presented in 5 common sense sections - getting ready, getting started, getting round, getting out of trouble, and getting it in the hole, this tape provides 50 golfing tips for novices and experts alike. Addresses key golfing questions such as “How can I avoid the slice”? “What do I need?” “What’s the best way to tackle the ball?”. As well as many others.
